creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: 6 Things to Know about AIE World's Fair 2026
videos.summary: AI Engineer announces AIE World's Fair 2026 in San Francisco, highlighting expanded content, expo floor experience, research-industry synergy, leadership tracks, and AI verticals focus. The video offers promotional discount codes and claims sponsor offers worth $40k for attendees.

videos.title: The Production AI Playbook: Deploying Agents at Enterprise Scale — Sandipan Bhaumik, Databricks
videos.summary: Sandipan Bhaumik from Databricks outlines five pillars for deploying agents at enterprise scale: evaluation, observability, data foundation, multi-agent orchestration, and governance. The talk draws from a case study of a retail bank whose chatbot PoC failed until systematic evaluation, tracing infrastructure, and measurement pipelines were prioritized before model selection.

videos.title: Your Agent's Biggest Lie: "I Searched the Web" — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
videos.summary: The talk demonstrates that AI agents often claim to have searched the web when they actually failed due to blocking, CAPTCHAs, or fake pages, then fall back to stale training data. It shows how proper web access via Bright Data's Web MCP changes agent behavior by enabling reliable citations, real-time results, and reducing hallucinations across sites like LinkedIn, Instagram, Amazon, and TikTok.

videos.title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps — Ziv Ilan, Nvidia
videos.summary: Ziv Ilan's NVIDIA team demonstrated a video diffusion model achieving near real-time generation on a single Blackwell B200 by reducing denoising steps from fifty through quantization, caching, and distillation techniques. The approach trains student models to match teacher outputs in four, eight, or one step using trajectory-based or distribution-based distillation strategies.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
videos.summary: Engineers at Anthropic and other organizations use automated loops that prompt AI agents repeatedly rather than manual prompting. These loops can run for extended periods unattended and involve orchestrators spawning parallel worker agents. The approach trades cost for automation and reliability gains.

videos.title: Omnigent: The New Meta-Harness for EVERY Coding Agent - Claude Code, Codex, Pi, More
videos.summary: Omnigent is an open-source meta-harness that orchestrates multiple coding agents like Claude Code, Codex, and Pi as interchangeable workers. It enables workflow patterns where different models handle different tasks such as implementation and review, with human-in-the-loop governance and cross-device session continuity.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
videos.summary: Cole Medin discusses the apparent US government-ordered shutdown of Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 model, citing an export-control directive related to claimed jailbreak vulnerabilities. The video examines why the model was pulled for all customers and covers performance benchmarks comparing Fable 5 to Claude Opus 4.8.

videos.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
videos.summary: Google's Agents CLI is an open-source command-line tool that simplifies building and deploying production-grade AI agents in minutes. It provides features including agent identity, sandboxed code execution, and audit trails, and works with any AI coding assistant like Claude Code.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: OpenClaw Creator's new secret project...
videos.summary: AI Jason announces a new project involving OpenClaw Creator, Crabbox, and a codebase harness available on GitHub. The video also promotes Superdesign and the AI Builder Club community for learning implementation details.

videos.title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real
videos.summary: A tutorial on Loop Engineer, a tool for constructing AI agents. The video includes setup instructions, a GitHub repository with templates and code harness, and references to a workshop in the AI Builder Club community.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: Video explains how to build proactive AI agents and self-improving company systems using autonomous loops. Covers proactive AI loop architecture, autonomous ads optimization and SEO use cases, and the role of memory and tools in enabling such systems. Demonstrates this with Loopany framework.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses the /goal feature and autonomous coding frameworks, comparing them to Ralph-loop 2.0 and exploring how tools like Crewlet enable agents to handle long horizontal tasks through mission-based exploration and goal-buddy functionality.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: GPT explained visually..
videos.summary: A visual educational overview of how GPT models work, covering self-attention, multi-head attention, feed-forward networks, positional embeddings, layer normalization, and residual connections. The video explains the fundamental mechanics of generative pretrained transformers that power models like Gemini, Opus, and Qwen.

videos.title: Karpathy Bigram explained in 10min..
videos.summary: This is an educational video explaining Andrej Karpathy's bigram language model approach. The video covers tokenization, embeddings, loss functions, softmax, and the AdamW optimizer as building blocks toward understanding GPT and attention mechanisms in large language models.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA's Nemotron 3 series includes Nano, Super, and Ultra variants that combine hybrid Mamba, Latent MoE, and MTP architectures. The video examines how these components differentiate Nemotron 3 from other open-source language models.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax released M3, a language model featuring MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA) that replaces full attention with sparse attention mechanisms. The model includes tiled I/O improvements and optimized KV cache reading for reduced latency during prefill and decode stages of inference.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: Your AI Agent Needs a Tool
videos.summary: AWS tutorial on enabling AI agents to use tools for real-time data and actions instead of relying solely on training data. Demonstrates tool architecture with schema and handlers, contrasts agents versus chatbots, and shows Python implementation using Strands Agents framework with MCP standard.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS is a framework that equips AI coding agents with tools, up-to-date documentation, best practices, and tested procedures for working with AWS services. It improves agent success rates and reduces inefficiency by providing reliable access to current AWS knowledge rather than relying on agents' general training data.

videos.title: Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
videos.summary: AWS Developers demonstrates running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock, enabling developers to execute Anthropic's agentic coding tool while keeping data within AWS infrastructure. The tutorial covers environment setup, cross-region inference, prompt caching, the Bedrock Mantle endpoint, cost tracking, and content governance with Bedrock Guardrails.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers discusses job-hunting strategies for tech professionals in 2026 when AI is screening resumes. The video covers networking, personal branding, the Feynman principle for learning, and argues that junior developers remain valuable despite AI advancement, using a transportation-industry analogy.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: A video from Alberta Tech examining whether Google engineers use "vibe coding" practices. It compares OpenAI Codex and Claude Code capabilities, surveys current vibing practices at Google in 2024, and speculates about future directions through 2026.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses Malus, a company or tool presented as disruptive to open source software development. The video examines how Malus functions, its impact on open source maintainers, and debates whether the company's approach is ethically problematic for the open source ecosystem.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: How Gong Built a $7B+ Company in the Age of Vibe Coding | Amit Bendov
videos.summary: Amit Bendov, CEO of Gong, argues that software moats depend on customer context, proprietary data, and operational complexity rather than code alone. He predicts that AI will not disrupt SaaS companies as dramatically as feared and advocates for building AI products that create raving fans rather than simply satisfied customers.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan, a Stanford roboticist, argues that the upcoming "ChatGPT moment" for robotics should prioritize design for human delight and interaction over pure utility and humanoid form factors. She contends that companies like 1X and Unitree pursuing humanoid robots may be pursuing a narrow vision, and that billions of robots entering society require rethinking beyond intelligence to include human-centered design principles.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Pigment is an AI performance management platform that integrates finance, supply chain, HR, and sales data into one unified system for decision-making. Founded by Eléonore Crespo, it has raised over $400 million and serves major customers including Figma, Brex, and Anthropic. The platform addresses the gap left by legacy spreadsheet-based planning at enterprises like Google.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that passionate early customers do not confirm real product-market fit, which instead manifests as customer pull rather than founder push. He advocates skipping formal customer interviews in favor of embedded relationships and warns against confusing strategic AI bets with convenient delusions.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: AI Could Delete EVERYTHING! Businesses Ignored This! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof argues that agentic AI poses overlooked security threats including prompt injection, data deletion, and unauthorized actions. He contends that businesses are hesitant to deploy agentic AI due to these risks despite its potential.

videos.title: AI Podcast: Anthropic's Latest & Lisbon's Electric Vibe #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof's podcast episode discusses Anthropic's latest developments and announcements. The episode features guest Joao and covers recent news from the AI company alongside broader cultural events.

videos.title: Small Teams vs. Big Teams: Productivity & Meeting Madness! #shorts
videos.summary: Small agile teams are more productive and efficient than large teams, which get bogged down in meetings and coordination overhead. The video argues that efficiency and task completion favor smaller organizational units.

videos.title: AI Memory Boom: Micron & SK Hynix Surge! Huge Growth Ahead! #shorts
videos.summary: The video discusses how AI demand is driving growth in high bandwidth memory products. Micron and SK Hynix are highlighted as companies benefiting from and shaping the AI memory infrastructure market.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
